page: Microsoft Azure B2s_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Bsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a resource density of 4.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as the stress-ng single-core CPU test at 1889.18 and the Geekbench single-core score of 1596.0, fall into the average tier. However, multi-core performance is limited, with the stress-ng multi-core test scoring 1932.68 and Geekbench multi-core scoring 1881.0, both categorized in the bottom 10%. Memory performance is also constrained; the large-block read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks reached 14.1 GB/sec and 13.38 GB/sec respectively, placing them in the lower performance brackets.

The burstable CPU allocation model makes this server suitable for workloads with variable CPU utilization. It is not designed for sustained high-compute tasks, as evidenced by low scores in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations. It is best suited for low-traffic web serving, small-scale background tasks, or development environments where CPU usage spikes occasionally.
description: Microsoft Azure B2s_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Bsv2 family, configured with 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz on an x86_64 architecture, this instance utilizes Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization. It features no local storage or GPU resources. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1596.0, but multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the lower percentiles. For example, the stress-ng multi-core score is 1932.68, and large-block memory read bandwidth is limited to 14.1 GB/sec. Given these hardware constraints and its burstable CPU allocation, this server is best suited for low-intensity workloads, such as light web serving, development environments, or small background processes that experience intermittent CPU utilization.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B2s_v2 is a burstable virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, designed for workloads with variable CPU utilization.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B2s_v2 is a burstable x86_64 virtual machine with 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8370C CPU, suited for light workloads.
tagline: A burstable, budget-friendly virtual machine with balanced memory-to-core allocation for workloads with intermittent CPU utilization.

Burstable CPU allocation for handling occasional performance spikes
Average single-core performance contrasted with limited multi-core capacity
No local storage or dedicated GPU resources included
Suited for development environments, light web serving, and low-intensity background tasks
